steps.title: Identify base angles in isosceles triangle
steps.content:
Since $AB = AC$, triangle $ABC$ is isosceles. In an isosceles triangle, the angles opposite the equal sides are equal. The sides $AB$ and $AC$ are equal, so the angles at $B$ and $C$ (the base angles) must be equal.

Therefore: $\angle ABC = \angle ACB = 67^{\circ}$

steps.title: Use triangle angle sum to find x
steps.content:
The sum of all angles in any triangle equals $180^{\circ}$:
$$\angle BAC + \angle ABC + \angle ACB = 180^{\circ}$$
$$x + 67^{\circ} + 67^{\circ} = 180^{\circ}$$
$$x + 134^{\circ} = 180^{\circ}$$
$$x = 46^{\circ}$$
That is the correct answer.
